title: Steamy Sweet Japanese Grape Tomatoes Ochazuke
description: A Japanese-inspired brunch dish featuring grape tomatoes and a creamy sauce, served in a unique corn husk wrap, this ochazuke is a twist on traditional Japanese comfort food, incorporating Mediterranean flavors for a fresh twist.
instructions: Prepare the corn husks by soaking them in water for 30 minutes to make them pliable.
In a large saucepan, combine the brewed green tea and heavy cream, and heat over low heat until warm.
Add the unsalted butter to the tea mixture and stir until melted.
Add the grated daikon radish and pickled ginger to the tea mixture and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked Japanese rice and the tea mixture, stirring until the rice is well coated.
Add the halved grape tomatoes to the rice mixture and stir gently to combine.
Season the rice mixture with salt and furikake to taste.
To assemble the ochazuke, lay a corn husk flat and place about 1/2 cup of the rice mixture in the center of the husk.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the filling and roll up the husk to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make 4-6 ochazuke.
Serve the ochazuke immediately, garnished with sesame seeds.
